summary_one_line: Input must begin with a recognized 4-byte SFNT tag (e.g. "typ1") at offset 0 so u.tag matches a non-collection case.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The first 4 bytes (offset 0x00-0x03) must equal a recognized non-collection SFNT signature tag such as Typ1Tag ('typ1' = 74 79 70 31), so that the switch on u.tag in get_face takes the case Typ1Tag/TrueTypeTag arm instead of falling to default.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed begins with the literal bytes 74 79 70 31 ('typ1'); the byte-diff shows W is fully concentrated on t/y/p/1 at offsets 0x00-0x03 (each 20/20), matching the Typ1Tag case.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry arbitrary leading bytes (00/e0/ff/87...) at offsets 0x00-0x03 that match no tag case, so the switch falls to default and returns Null, blocking the true side (L hits 491 default, never 487).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the tag comparison and record both operands in the CmpLogObserver buffer; I2SRandReplace then substitutes the expected tag literal (Typ1Tag/etc.) into the input at the matching offset, planting the 4-byte magic that naive/value_profile must hit by blind havoc.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the case Typ1Tag arm of a switch on u.tag inside get_face, whose true side is globally blocked.
The branch dispatches on the 4-byte tag value u.tag with sibling tag cases and a default fallthrough.
All five winner-resolving seeds start with the literal bytes 74 79 70 31 spelling 'typ1'.
At offsets 0x00-0x03 the winner bytes are fully concentrated on t/y/p/1 (20 of 20) while loser bytes are a scattered set of other values.
Loser-blocking seeds carry arbitrary leading bytes that do not spell a tag, e.g. a seed beginning 87 0e 0d 0e 22 0e.
In the loser the switch falls to the default arm (line 491 returns Null) rather than the Typ1Tag arm.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 491  T=0 F=755  T=768 F=0  default:		return Null (OpenTypeFontFace);

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which reads the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the other operand at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs differ from their loser by exactly the I2S technique.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5389 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 15af638beaf4cc6d` and observed 1 I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 3 of the ancestor chain, the lower-bound signal of an I2SRandReplace find under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where naive/value_profile resolve the Typ1Tag case at the same rate as cmplog/vpc despite the gate requiring a 4-byte exact tag match, showing I2S substitution is not what plants the magic bytes.

weakest_evidence_point: The cmplog winner's lineage shows only one dash row (depth 3) and the leaf op-list is havoc/token, so the I2S contribution is a lower bound and could partly be lucky havoc.
